Lens sit second in the Ligue 1 standings on 59 points, one behind PSG.

However, the French giants have a game in hand and could extend their lead to four points with seven fixtures to go.

Since the 2012/13 campaign, PSG have failed to win Ligue 1 just twice, cementing their status as the top dogs in France.

It is why Lens, who finished runners-up in Ligue 1 only three seasons ago, won't give up on preserving the original date of the PSG fixture without a fight.

Football.title: Jurgen Klopp rubbishes Real Madrid links but names surprise job he would prefer
Football.content:
Jurgen Klopp rubbishes Real Madrid links but names surprise job he would prefer

Jürgen Klopp has emphatically shut down rumours around him potentially becoming the next Real Madrid manager.

The former Liverpool boss has been linked with Real since they sacked Xabi Alonso in January, but he's rubbished the speculation.

3 Klopp has shut down talk of him becoming the next Real Madrid manager Credit: AFP

However, Klopp, who has been on a break since leaving Anfield in 2024, has left the door open to an eventual return to management.



He is currently Head of Global Soccer at Red Bull, working with teams including RB Leipzig, RB Salzburg and Leeds United.

But it doesn't seem like he'll be leaving that role too soon, as he boldly shut down the talk about him and Real Madrid.

Speaking at a press conference after being unveiled as a pundit for German channel Magenta TV at this summer's World Cup, he said: “All the news that’s coming out is complete nonsense.

“They haven’t called me even once, not even a single time! They haven’t called my agent either.



“Preferably, I’d take over at Atletico Madrid at the same time. Sorry, Madrid, you’ll have to call first”.

Klopp revealed that he has had no contact with at all with Real Madrid, who currently have Alvaro Arbeloa in charge.



And the German's passing mention of their bitter rivals Atletico Madrid may indicate what side of Madrid he'd rather head to.

Diego Simeone has been Atletico boss for the last 15 years and is a club icon, with his contract running through to 2027.



But for now, he's planning on staying as part of the Red Bull team.

3 The ex-Liverpool boss won't be joining Real and joked about Atletico Credit: AFP

Returning to the touchline?

After shutting down the Real Madrid rumours, Klopp was asked whether he would ever come back to management.

And he admitted: "I'm certainly quite advanced for life at my age, but as a coach, I'm not completely and utterly finished.

"I haven't reached retirement age yet.

"Who knows what will happen in the coming years. But nothing is planned in that regard."

Klopp also addressed links to the Germany national team job, but with the World Cup coming up this summer, he said: "At the moment, I'm obviously not thinking about that at all.



"And fortunately, there's no reason to."

3 Klopp is heading back to Anfield this weekend as an assistant in a legends match Credit: Getty

Klopp back at Liverpool

Klopp may not be planning to return to management in the near future, but he will be back on the touchline at Anfield soon.

He will be an assistant to Liverpool legend Sir Kenny Dalglish for a legends match between the Reds and Borussia Dortmund.

The 58-year-old is going to be back on Merseyside this Saturday to help raise money for the LFC Foundation.

Klopp spent nine years as Liverpool manager, winning eight trophies, including the Premier League and Champions League.



And before that, he'd spent seven years at Dortmund, winning two German Bundesliga titles with them.

So, it will be a meaningful clash and enough to see him make a comeback to the Anfield dugout this weekend.

Football.title: Arsenal handed ‘used to choking’ warning by Noel Gallagher as Man City cup triumph threatens to shift title race dial
Football.content:
Arsenal handed ‘used to choking’ warning by Noel Gallagher as Man City cup triumph threatens to shift title race dial

Noel Gallagher has warned Arsenal that their loss in the Carabao Cup final could lead to them 'choking' in the title race.

The Gunners went into the showpiece against Manchester City on Sunday filled with confidence that the trophy would be theirs.

4 Arsenal's quadruple hopes were ended by Man City in the Carabao Cup final on Sunday Credit: Getty

But Pep Guardiola got the better of his former assistant Mikel Arteta at Wembley, with a double by Nico O'Reilly securing the trophy for City.



And Arteta's six-year wait for a second major trophy as manager continued, in what may end up being a huge blow for the club.

Arsenal still remain nine points clear of City at the top of the Premier League with seven games to play, while they are also in the Champions League and FA Cup quarter-finals.

So, there is a chance that they could pick up a historic treble despite tasting disappointment in their first final of the campaign.

However, Oasis legend and Man City fan Gallagher believes that the Gunners could well be impacted by the loss which could benefit City.



Gallagher told talkSPORT Drive on Monday: "That game for Arsenal was enormous, they must have spoken about it in the week saying: 'If we win this trophy, it's the first for this team'.

"But now they've given everyone in the country the chance to call them chokers and bottlers again.



"And when it comes down to it, in a few weeks' time, when there's a game they have to win, then they're used to choking, so we'll see.

"Even in the Champions League, it doesn't bode well...

"Let's just say City and Arsenal get to the FA Cup final, they're lining up again and looking across the halfway line thinking... they don't seem to be the type of team to take it by the scruff of the neck in a one-off game."



4 Gallagher thinks Arsenal are likely to hold on, but that concerns could now rise Credit: AFP

4 The Gunners are nine points clear in the Premier League with seven games to go

"I don't think Arsenal can be caught, but they've been shot in the thigh," he added. "We'll see what they're made of.

"City have to win every game now this season... it might be too much, but it bodes well for next season."

A mental block?

Gallagher also questioned whether the Arsenal players are made for playing in big, important games, unlike City's.



"I don't know what was said in the dressing room, but Pep had them fired up... it looked like City wanted it more than Arsenal," he said.

"After the second goal Arsenal threw it in, they were looking around like, 'just get us off the pitch'.

"At no point from the second half onwards did it feel like we were going to lose that game. Their end emptied rapid and the players didn't want it."

4 Arsenal were widely criticised for their approach after conceding to Man City Credit: Shutterstock Editorial

He continued: "This is becoming a habit now for Arsenal.

"The only time they've turned up is when they spanked us 5-1 and other than that, you hear it from Arsenal fans all the time: 'We never turned up'.



"I don't know whether that's City not allowing them to play their game, or maybe they've got a mental block."

Arsenal will be thrown straight back into a crucial game after the international break with their FA Cup quarter-final away at in-form Southampton on April 4.

And then just three days after that, the first leg of their Champions League quarter-final against Sporting Lisbon follows.

They also face Bournemouth, Sporting again, Man City in a title race six-pointer, and Newcastle in April - with the next few weeks set to prove whether the Gunners have what it takes.

Football.title: Steve Cooper accused of dropping key player on purpose ahead of Wales’ World Cup play-off
Football.content:
Steve Cooper has been accused of dropping a Brondby player on purpose by Bosnia-Herzegovina manager Sergej Barbez.

The former Premier League boss opted not to play Bosnian midfielder Benjamin Tahirovic ahead of the World Cup play-offs, where Bosnia face Wales.

3 The ex-Premier League boss has been accused of dropping a player on purpose Credit: Getty

And for making that decision, Cooper, who is Welsh, has taken some flak from Barbez before the crucial Thursday night meeting.



Craig Bellamy's Dragons take on Bosnia in their play-off semi-final with only the winning team keeping their hopes of playing at the World Cup this summer alive.

But their manager thinks that Cooper has now done his nation a favour by reducing the minutes of one of his key players at Brondby.

The ex-Nottingham Forest and Leicester City boss hasn't selected Tahirovic to start any of his side's last four matches, with the 23-year-old not making the squad at all in the previous two.

That led to Barbez saying in a press conference on Monday: “Benjo has told me some things that are hard to believe. It has something to do with his coach’s roots and origins.



“When your coach wishes you, but not your national team, good luck, everything is much clearer.

“He [Cooper] has told him that everything will return to normal after the national team season.



"I am not like that, I love and value sport and competition more.”

talkSPORT has contacted Cooper's representatives for comment about Barbez's claims.

Tahirovic, who has 24 caps for Bosnia, is considered a star player for Brondby, but has been regularly left out by Cooper. However, the 46-year-old has previously explained the decision - and it's nothing to do with the fact he will be facing Wales.



3 Tahirovic will likely be lining up for Bosnia against Wales in their World Cup play-off this week Credit: Getty

What has Cooper said?

Cooper explained why he had been leaving Tahirovic out of his Brondby squad in recent weeks when speaking on March 19.

And it appears to be in regard to a disciplinary problem.

He said to Danish outlet TV 2 Sport: "It's a shame. Such situations are never good, but unfortunately, it's the right thing to do.

"The team, the club, the traditions and the values ​​are always the most important thing.

"I'm not going to go into it more specifically on a personal level.

"But when you see things that don't live up to the values ​​and the team, you have to do something about it."



3 Cooper has already explained why Tahirovic hasn't been playing Credit: AFP

Brondby respond

On top of Cooper's previous reasoning, Brondy have also now spoken out against Barbez's allegations of dropping a player on purpose.

Their communications chief, Soren Hanghoj, told TV 2 Sport: “This is quite a far-reaching speculation.

"Steve has publicly stated the considerations behind the decision — and none of them have the slightest connection with either national team. That goes without saying.

“It is not just a head coach who is the sponsor of a decision like the one in question here.



"It is a club decision that has been made jointly by an entire coaching team and the sporting management.

"And there are not that many Wales fans in Brondby after all.”

Football.title: Enzo Fernandez open to leaving Chelsea as dream move to European giants revealed
Football.content:
Enzo Fernandez open to leaving Chelsea as dream move to European giants revealed

Enzo Fernandez is open to a move to Real Madrid this summer, talkSPORT understands.

talkSPORT has learned a transfer to the Bernabeu would be viewed as a dream move for the Chelsea vice-captain.

3 The Argentinian star could soon land Chelsea a major transfer windfall Credit: Getty

Real have not yet made a formal move or started any talks as they continue to shortlist midfield targets for the summer.

When will Fernandez's future be resolved?

This was also confirmed by Argentine World Cup winner when asked about his future while on international duty with Argentina.

“We’ll see after the World Cup,” said Fernandez when asked about his future.

“We've had zero talks as of now. Honestly, nothing yet [with Real Madrid]. Now I am focused on Chelsea and what’s left in the last few games this season.”

Which other clubs could try to sign Fernandez?

Fernandez has also been linked with a move to Paris Saint-Germain and Saudi Arabia.

talkSPORT understands his preference is a switch to LaLiga, and that PSG have not taken any steps to try and sign the Chelsea midfielder despite several reports.

Fernandez does have genuine interest from Al Ittihad but is not open to Saudi Arabia at this stage of his career.

Why is Fernandez open to a move away from Chelsea?

Chelsea don’t want to lose Fernandez and he has not directly told the club he wishes to leave.

However, talkSPORT has learned Fernandez was disappointed by the manner of Enzo Maresca’s exit and is actively exploring options without outright agitating for a transfer away.

Fernandez wishes to play Champions League football with regularity and how he approaches the summer may be influenced by whether Chelsea qualify again for that tournament.

3 Fernandez's time at Stamford Bridge could be coming to a close Credit: Getty

Chelsea sources remain calm about the situation.

Fernandez is contracted until 2032 having already had a club-option on his long-term contract triggered.

Chelsea not only view Fernandez as not for sale but are willing to offer the 25-year-old fresh terms.

Some initial conversations over a new deal have begun but the parties are not advanced or close to any agreement.

Chelsea signed Fernandez from Benfica in January 2023 for a then British transfer record £107million, and even if the Blues were to U-turn on their current stance, no sale would be sanctioned for a book loss.

Given Chelsea hold all the cards, that makes it complicated for Fernandez to leave Chelsea this summer, but the player is nonetheless open to doing so if Real Madrid come calling.

3 Fernandez was the British record signing up until last summer Credit: AFP

Fernandez first stoked the flames of a potential summer departure from Chelsea when he couldn't say for certain he'd remain at Stamford Bridge during an interview after a 3-0 loss to PSG in Champions League action.

The result in the second leg completed an 8-2 defeat on aggregate to the Parisians in the round of 16.

Liam Rosenior responds to Fernandez speculation

Blues boss Liam Rosenior was reluctant to speak on Fernadnez's revelation immediately after the match, but later confirmed he had held talks with the Argentina international who reaffirmed his commitment to the club.

"I had a great conversation with Enzo at length this morning at the training ground," Rosenior said last Thursday.

"Not just about the comments, but the future and how much we want to win the coming games.

"I would say he made it really clear how happy he is here, how much he wants to win for the team, and how passionate he is for this football club.

"He also said the translation and emotion got misconstrued. He is completely committed to this football club."

Football.title: Wales vs Bosnia-Herzegovina LIVE: Date, kick-off time, team news and where to watch World Cup play-off clash
Football.content:
Wales vs Bosnia-Herzegovina LIVE: Date, kick-off time, team news and where to watch World Cup play-off clash

Wales must win their two play-off matches to secure their place at the World Cup in North America this summer.

And their all-or-nothing journey begins with a tough semi-final against Bosnia-Herzegovina later this week in Cardiff.

3 Wales have to win this game to keep their hopes of playing at the World Cup alive Credit: Getty

Craig Bellamy's side were unable to secure automatic qualification for the tournament after finishing second in their group.



So, it has meant they have had an agonising wait for the play-offs, being drawn against Bosnia, who also finished second in qualifying.

The winner of this tie will then take on the winner of the other play-off semi-final between Northern Ireland and Italy.

And Wales will be dreaming of now setting up a date with destiny on March 31, with a World Cup place on the line.

Wales vs Bosnia-Herzegovina: Date and where to watch

This World Cup play-off tie will take place on Thursday, March 26.

Kick-off at the Principality Stadium in Cardiff is scheduled for 7:45pm.



The game will be broadcast free-to-air in the UK this week.

Fans will be able to watch on BBC Two or BBC One Wales.

talkSPORT and talkSPORT.com will also keep you up to date with all the World Cup qualifying action.

Natalie Sawyer will have all the updates on the Kick Off on talkSPORT.



3 The Dragons must come out on top of both of their play-off games to qualify Credit: Getty

To tune in to talkSPORT or talkSPORT 2 through the website, click HERE for the live stream.



You can also listen via the talkSPORT app, on DAB digital radio, through your smart speaker and on 1089 or 1053 AM.

Wales vs Bosnia-Herzegovina: Squads

Wales will hope that Harry Wilson can continue his fine Premier League form on the international stage this week.

Meanwhile, other top-flight stars such as Ethan Ampadu, Joe Rodon and Neco Williams are going to have to step up.

Captain Aaron Ramsey has not been called up, with Ben Davies, Kieffer Moore and Chris Mepham missing out injured.

Wales squad: Karl Darlow, Danny Ward, Adam Davies, Tom King, Norrington-Davies, Jay DaSilva, Dylan Lawlor, Ben Cabango, Joe Rodon, Neco Williams, Ronan Kpakio, Ethan Ampadu, Josh Sheehan, Jordan James, Liam Cullen, Joel Colwill, Harry Wilson, David Brooks, Nathan Broadhead, Brennan Johnson, Sorba Thomas, Mark Harris, Lewis Koumas, Dan James, Rabbi Matondo, Rubin Colwill.



3 Bellamy knows his side have to be wary of ex-Premier League star Dzeko Credit: Getty

As for Bosnia, their squad includes ex-Arsenal defender Sead Kolasinac and former Manchester City striker Edin Dzeko.

Bosnia-Herzegovina squad: Nikola Vasilj, Martin Zlomislic, Osma Hadzikic, Sead Kolasinac, Dennis Hadziakadunic, Amar Dedic, Nikola Katic, Tarik Muharemovic, Nihad Mujakic, Stjepan Radeljic, Nidal Celik, Amir Hadziametovic, Benjamin Tahirovic, Armin Gigovic, Dzenis Burnic, Ivan Basic, Amar Memic, Ivan Sunjic, Edin Dzeko, Ermedin Demirovic, Esmir Bajraktarevic, Samed Bazdar, Haris Tabakovic, Kerim Alajbegovic, Jovo Lukic.

Wales vs Bosnia-Herzegovina: What has been said?

Wales boss Bellamy discussed the threat of Dzeko and Bosnia after his side were drawn against them in the World Cup play-offs.

"The idea for us would be to keep Bosnia away from our box because that's when he comes into his own," he said.



"Watching Bosnia has been a decent watch so far.

"It's going to be a tough game. Physically, they are very good, well-organised [and] good in both boxes. They don't need too many chances to score."

Football.title: Premier League clubs on red alert with Bayern Munich open to selling Alphonso Davies
Football.content:
Bayern Munich full-back Alphonso Davies could be available in the summer transfer window with Manchester United one of the clubs interested.

Davies has been immense for Bayern since his breakout season in 2019/20 and supported the team with 34 assists and 14 goals in 239 appearances.

3 Davies has assisted two goals in all competitions this season Credit: Getty

He has won six Bundesliga titles during his career with the German giants and became the first Canadian to win the Champions League crown in 2019/20.



However, this season Davies has only made eight league appearances due to multiple injuries.

Last season, he suffered a cruciate ligament tear which seen him miss 29 games in a row. Since returning this year he has been sidelined on four separate occasions.

The 25-year-old only signed a new contract with Bayern last season, which runs until 2030.

But according to Bayern reporter Christian Falk, the club could consider moving Davies on this summer if any clubs come to the table with a reasonable offer.



It is suggested Manchester United are interested in the left-back after they have had their own concerns over squad depth in that specific position.

Luke Shaw has experienced a lack of long-term competition in his role at Old Trafford. That's despite a dreadful injury record which has seen him miss over 330 games since joining Man United.



The ex-Southampton player was sat out for the majority of last season with calf and muscle injuries.

Additionally, Tyrell Malacia is likely to leave Old Trafford which will leave the versatile Patrick Dorgu as the only option to cover at full-back.



Falk told CF Bayern Insider: "It is true, Manchester United are interested in Alphonso Davies.

3 Shaw was England's first-choice left-back when fit for years - but Tuchel is yet to pick him for England Credit: Getty

"They were at the table when questions were being asked as to whether he would sign a new contract at Bayern Munich.

"They’re still monitoring this player, but he’s, of course, since signed a new contract at the Allianz Arena, so it will be difficult to get him at the moment.

"But there are also discussions going on at the club as to whether they need a new back-up player, given all the injuries Davies keeps picking up.



"Before, they thought they didn’t need a player on the left-hand side because Hiroki Ito can play there as well.

"However, if there were an offer on the table (and don’t forget it cost Bayern a lot of money to extend Davies), there would be a discussion.

"But I can say right now that there has yet to be a concrete offer from Manchester United.

Alphonso Davies' injuries in 2025/26 Fitness, From: 31/10/25 - 08/12/2025 (39 days)

Illness, From: 04/01/26 - 15/01/2026 (12 days)

Torn muscle fiber, From 22/02/2026 - 09/03/26 (16 days)

Hamstring strain, From 11/03/2026 - Ongoing

3 Despite the lack of games played this season, Davies is on his way to a seventh title with Bayern Credit: Getty

"If there’s an offer, perhaps Bayern will consider it, as the injuries keep coming."



Davies can be a world-class player but his consistent injuries are proving to be a headache for the Bundesliga giants.

United are looking at him but they are also exploring other options, with reports of interest in Myles Lewis-Skelly from Arsenal.

News.title: Massive explosion rocks Texas oil refinery as flames & plumes of smoke spew out of depot after locals heard ‘loud boom’
News.content:
UP IN FLAMES

UP IN FLAMES Massive explosion rocks Texas oil refinery as flames & plumes of smoke spew out of depot after locals heard ‘loud boom’

A LARGE explosion at an oil refinery has shot plumes of smoke into the air and left nearby residents seeking shelter.

Authorities have urged locals to stay inside as firefighters work to control the aftermath of the blast at the Valero refinery in Port Arthur, on the Texas coast.





The refinery is an estimated 90 miles east of Houston, according to Mayor Charlotte M. Moses.

Images of the blast have popped up online, showing large smoke clouds rising upwards from the plant.

Flames could also be seen billowing from the warehouse buildings.

Residents in the surrounding areas reported hearing a loud boom and seeing their windows shake.

READ MORE WORLD NEWS BLAST SHOCK Four hospitalized in cannabis oil refinery explosion felt across THREE states

Sheriff Zena Stephens said the explosion was likely caused by an industrial heater.

There have been no reported injuries.

“There’s been an explosion, yes, but we’re OK; everybody’s OK,” Mayor Moses said.

“[Firefighters are] trying to put the fire out as quickly as possible.”



The City of Port Arthur has posted updated advice for locals on its Facebook page.

“For your safety please remain in place until the ‘All Clear’ is given by emergency personnel,” the statement said.

Texas state Representative Christian Manuel said the Texas Commission on Environmental Quality had arrived at the refinery with air monitoring equipment and was working with local and state partners.

In a post to social media he also urged residents to be cautious as the situation continued to unfold.

“Please limit outdoor activity, keep windows and doors closed, and follow guidance from local officials,” he said.



The explosion comes as global gas prices surge, driven by uncertainty over the world’s oil supply.

The refinery employs about 770 people.

It can process an estimated 435,000 barrels of oil every day, according to Valero’s website.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

The plant refines heavy sour crude oil into gasoline, diesel and jet fuel.

News.title: Murder probe launched after woman, 88, died at care home in ‘suspicious’ circumstances as 2 OAPs arrested
News.content:
CARE HOME HORROR Murder probe launched after woman, 88, died at care home in ‘suspicious’ circumstances as 2 OAPs arrested

COPS have launched a murder investigation after an elderly woman died in “suspicious circumstances” at a care home.

Two pensioners have been arrested after the victim, 88, was discovered dead at the home in Sleaford, Lincolnshire.



Officers received reports of a death at Sleaford Manor Care Home in the early hours of Sunday.

Emergency crews were scrambled to the care home at around 2am, where they discovered the body of an 88-year-old woman.



A man, aged 74, and a woman, aged 75, were initially arrested in connection with the incident.

Lincolnshire Police have since confirmed the pair have been released from custody.

Issuing an update last night, the force said a murder probe had been launched following a post-mortem.

Detective Inspector Mel Rooke, of the Major Crime Unit, said: “Our thoughts are with the family and friends of the victim this evening.



“Our specially trained officers will be providing support to her family as our inquiry continues.

“I understand any unexpected death is a concern to our communities and this tragic death will be no different.

“Our inquiries will take some time to complete and I would ask that the family and the care home are left in peace and their privacy is respected.

“I can say there is no wider risk to the public.”



A spokesperson for Care UK, which runs the care home, said it would be cooperating with the police investigation.

They told LincolnshireLive: “We are deeply saddened by this incident and send our condolences to the resident’s family.

“The incident is now part of an active police investigation so it would be wrong for us to comment any further at this time.

“We will be cooperating with the investigation in any way we can.”

News.title: I feared I had HIV after my monster dad plied me with drugs & raped me at 14… move to escape twisted past sickened me
News.content:
AT the age of 14, Lauren Sullivan suffered nine months of sexual abuse at the hands of her father, Shaun Dallisson.

But, even after her dad was jailed and put on the sex offenders’ register, he made a cynical move which left her horrified – and gave him the potential to abuse again.



Lauren, 26, discovered her dad had changed his name by deed poll.

This means if people google the new name, nothing about his sordid past will come up and she worries that it won’t have been correctly linked to his old identity, allowing him to pass DBS checks he should fail.



Now she is worried that the shelving of a proposed bill, which had been progressing through parliament, will allow more sex offenders to fall off the radar.

Della’s Law, which aimed to prevent sex offenders from changing their names, reached parliament two years ago but has been dropped since Labour came to power, leaving campaigners feeling betrated.

Worse, instead of offenders being barred from a new moniker, those on the register now have LONGER to inform cops if they swap their name.

The new Crime and Policing Bill 2025 allows sick offenders seven days to notify cops of an intent to change name, rather than informing them within three days of the change, which Lauren fears will give them longer to slip off the radar.

“For survivors, this move from the government is completely in the wrong direction,” she said.



“Extending the notification window isn’t strengthening safeguarding at all. It’s the opposite of what we’ve been asking for all these years.

“It’s a kick in the teeth.”

Brave Lauren, who has waived her right to anonymity, only met her estranged father, Dallisson, when she was 11 years old, after he was diagnosed with HIV.

He assaulted her for nine months, often claiming he had come off his HIV medication to intimidate her.

Dallisson was jailed for eight years for his offences and signed the sex offenders register indefinitely in 2014.



But in 2022 Lauren discovered he had changed his name – something Lauren believes is a deliberate attempt to evade his past.

She said: “Allowing sex offenders to change their name is allowing them to evade their past and what they’ve done.

“Plus it is on them to notify police that they’ve done it, and it’s likely many won’t bother.

“It’s not reassuring for survivors and gives loopholes that they might not get flagged as an offender on a DBS check or appear on a Claire’s Law search or Sarah’s Law search.

“They could use it as a way to get access to vulnerable people again, and that means the system to protect people is failing.”



Lauren also raised concerns about those who do report their name changes not having the two identities linked correctly, which causes the same concerns.

Missing sex beasts

In 2023 the BBC discovered that 729 sex offenders had gone missing or were wanted for arrest after being placed on the sex offender register.

The same Freedom of Information request also found that 1,500 sex offenders had notified police forces of lawful name changes. At the time there were approximately 67,000 registered sex offenders in the UK.



In 2019, The Sun revealed that many offenders spend less than a year under monitoring requirements, with some being given just 69 days on the register for vile offences.

A Freedom of Information request around offenders being able to request removal from the register after 15 years found that hundreds had been successful.

Only those who committed serious offences are given life on the register but can apply for removal every 15 years.

The probe also found that 20 offenders who were removed from the register, at their own request, reoffended between 2015 and 2019.

Groomed as a teen

Dallisson left Lauren’s mum when she was pregnant, only reconnecting with his daughter after discovering he was HIV positive.



For the first three years of their relationship, there was no abuse, but Lauren believes he purposely waited three years to build up trust before grooming her.

He started by treating her like an adult with few rules and plying her with drugs and alcohol.

Lauren said: “I kind of had this idealistic idea of what a dad would be and kind of fantasised as a child about what my dad was like.

No survivor should have to worry about their abuser being able to fly under the radar because of a name change Lauren Sullivan

“I was always hoping that he would come back, and he definitely played on that.

“He also used my parents being separated to groom me as well. He’d tell me my mum didn’t understand what it was like to be a teenager, that she didn’t give me enough freedom, that she was too strict and so on.



“He was constantly positioning me against my mum when she was the only person that actually protected me and cared about me.”

Lauren was just 14 when Dallisson started his campaign of abuse, starting with assaulting her when she fell asleep watching a film.

“I woke up to his hands down my trousers. I was just kind of frozen. I had no idea what to do or think,” she explains.

What is Della's Law Della’s law was started by Della Wright, who was abused at age six by Terry Price, who had since changed his name to Robert McEwan. Ahead of his trial in 2016, he changed his name to Mr Mac, disrupting the legal system as the charges were for Mr McEwan. The trial eventually went ahead. He was sentenced to 22 years in prison with a five-year extension after being found guilty of sex offences involving children. Price went on to change his name and then offend again. In total he changed his name five times while conducting a series of offences over three decades. Supporters of Della’s law are calling for it to be illegal for convicted sex offenders to change their names. In 2020, Della told Sky News: “I just thought how many relationships has he had? How many jobs has he had around children? We don’t know but we know he’s a prolific offender. It makes me really angry and it shouldn’t be possible. “This person has been through the system not once but countless times – and even as recently as 2016 he was allowed to change his name again whilst in prison.”

“He stopped because I was crying, and he realised I wasn’t asleep.

“He was immediately so apologetic; he described himself as a monster, and that actually made me feel really guilty.”



It was on a trip to Egypt that year that he escalated the abuse.

“This was the first time he had full intercourse with me,” she says.

“My nan and my brother were in the next room, and I just lay there frozen with fear. I couldn’t believe what was happening.

“Now when I look back at the photos of me swimming with dolphins and looking so happy, it is horrifying to think I had no idea what would happen just a day later.”

‘No remorse’



Dallisson’s abuse came to light when Lauren’s mother discovered messages on her daughter’s phone and realised what had been going on. She reported him to the police.

Dallisson pleaded guilty to the offences in 2014, meaning his sentence was halved, and Lauren believed it showed remorse, but since he changed his name, she believes it was all a ploy for less jail time.

“As a teenager I really held on to him pleading guilty as meaning he had remorse,” she said.

“I was still heavily influenced by his grooming, I think.

“Then as an adult I opted to go through restorative justice – where you eventually meet with your abuser.



“But all that highlighted for me was that he is not remorseful for anything he’s done. It was an outward remorse for show.

“He pulled out of meeting me, claiming he was rebuilding his life, and all I wanted was to talk to the press. It showed me that he didn’t want to make amends or anything even close to it.

“Added to him changing his name, it tells me that he’s trying to avoid people knowing what he has done.”

Lauren has opted to share her story on TikTok, with some of her videos about her dad’s name change going viral.

Since posting, she’s heard from many other abuse survivors of their offenders opting to change their names as well.



In the UK, a deed poll request isn’t dealt with by the government; instead, it is done by private companies. To notify the government, people have to pay just over £53 to have their name changed on public record via the High Court.

If it’s not reported to the High Court or police, it is likely that their new name won’t show anything untoward on a DBS check.

She said: “I worry that the names aren’t properly linked if they do disclose changes to police, and that should never be an option.

“Della’s law would stop there being any worries around name changes because it would make it illegal, and there should be sanctions.

“No survivor should have to worry about their abuser being able to fly under the radar because of a name change.”

News.title: Moment roaring fire rips through moors with flames seen for miles & locals told ‘stay away’
News.content:
BLAZE HORROR Moment roaring fire rips through moors with flames seen for miles & locals told ‘stay away’

A MAJOR wildfire has ripped through a popular moorland – with thick plumes of smoke and flames visible from miles away.

Locals have been warned to stay away as fire crews continue to battle the roaring blaze near Rochdale, Greater Manchester.





Emergency crews were scrambled to the blaze – which broke out near a wind farm at Scout Moor – just before 5.45pm yesterday.

It is not yet known how the fire started, but teams have been battling through the night to extinguish it.

Five fire engines, a drone and a wildfire unit have been dispatched to help tackle the roaring flames.



Residents in Eccles – roughly 19 miles away – have reported seeing the fire.

Lancashire Fire and Rescue Service crews are being supported by units from Greater Manchester Fire and Rescue.

Issuing a statement yesterday, the service said: “We are currently in attendance at a wildfire under Scout Moor wind turbines.

“Several fire engines and our drone team are on scene, working with Greater Manchester crews to bring the fire under control



“We urge members of the public to stay away from the area while firefighters carry out their work.

“Your cooperation helps us manage the incident safely and effectively.”

Pictures from the scene showed Greater Manchester Police teams were also in attendance.

Scout Moor is the second largest onshore wind farm in England, operating 26 wind turbines.

It was commissioned in 2008 and, between 2009-2013, generated enough energy to power 200,000 homes.

News.title: Gran, 81, fined £270 after being unable to pay at broken parking meter as she vows not to be ‘bullied’ by court threats
News.content:
PARKING WARS Gran, 81, fined £270 after being unable to pay at broken parking meter as she vows not to be ‘bullied’ by court threats

A GRAN has been fined £270 after being unable to pay for parking because the meter was broken.

Krystyna Allen, 81, left her car parked for just 11 minutes in July last year before moving it because she was unable to pay at the broken machine.





Just 10 days after the pensioner left her car in Stratford-upon-Avon, Warwickshire, she received a fine for £100.

Krystyna said she had sent several letters to Smart Parking disputing the fine.

She added that she had explained that she tried to pay some three or four times using both cash and card.

Despite her best efforts however, she was unable to cover the cost of her parking as the machine was out of order.



In response to her letters, Krystyna claims, she has only received more demands for payment from the company.

The former primary school teacher’s original fine of £100 has now risen to a staggering £270.

The pensioner said she had even received letters from debt collectors and has been threatened with court proceedings amid the ongoing dispute.

Krystyna said: “I didn’t have a mobile with me, I don’t use one. Two very kind gentlemen confirmed it wasn’t working by trying their own cards.



“As I’d already been quite a few minutes, they suggested I’d better move my car before I was fined.

“I got back in the car and went and parked across the road at the Arts House car park so I could unload my food that I was offering for a singing event at the church next door.”

Krystyna said the 11 minutes between her arriving and leaving was because she was slow on her feet.

The pensioner, who is from nearby Langley remains determined that she will not be paying the car parking fine.

She branded the debt “outrageous” and vowed that she wouldn’t be bullied into backing down.



Krystyna said she had paid hundreds to park at that car park over the years.

She added that she would never think of parking there without paying.

According to Smart Parking the company’s systems can “handle all payment methods”, The Daily Telegraph reports.

The Sun has contacted Smart Parking for comment.

News.title: Useless French get WORSE at stopping Channel migrants this year, damning figures show
News.content:
THE French got worse at stopping the small boats in the first quarter of this year, figures show.

And the second week of this month was one of their most dismal performances yet, halting just 68 migrants while 357 arrived.





The damning figures come as Home Office negotiators fly to Paris to renew a £500million migration security deal.

We revealed yesterday how Home Secretary Shabana Mahmood had demanded “more bang for our buck” from her counterparts in France ahead of the 11th-hour negotiations.

But Sun analysis of Government data shows how authorities patrolling the beaches of Calais have had a far worse start to 2026 than 2025.

In the first 11 weeks of last year, 4,395 migrants arrived, while 3,227 were stopped — a ratio of 1.36 to one.



But this year, the ratio has surged to 1.85, with 3,457 arriving and just 1,865 stopped.

And the week to March 15 was one of the authorities’ worst yet, with 357 arriving and 68 stopped.

Shadow Home Secretary Chris Philp said: “The blame for the increase should lie squarely at Shabana Mahmood’s door.”

A Home Office spokesman said: “We do not recognise these calculations.



“France is our most important partner in tackling illegal migration.

“Our joint work is bearing down on crossings.

“Since this Government took office, 40,000 crossing attempts by migrants have been stopped and 60,000 illegal migrants with no right to be here have been removed or deported.”

News.title: Synagogues attacked, kids intimidated and ambulances torched… I’m sick of all the empty promises to oppose anti-Semitism
News.content:
WE woke up yesterday to the shocking news four ambulances operated by the Hatzola charity had been torched in North London.

Hatzola volunteers provide a vital, lifesaving service to the whole community — Jews and non-Jews alike — but it is clear it was targeted because it is a Jewish charity.





Last night, there were suspicions that Iran was behind the firebombing.

Government ministers queued up to condemn the attack.

The BBC broadcast live from the scene.

It was the same response when Jewish people were killed after a terrorist attacked Heaton Park Synagogue last year.



But what can anyone expect after the explosion of anti-Jewish hatred we’ve seen over the last few years?

When will authorities get a grip of the root causes?

Antisemites and Islamist extremists have targeted Jewish people — and the authorities let them get away with it.

Ever since Hamas attacked Israel in 2023, huge hate marches have been allowed to take over city centres.



Some were the usual woke students taking up the latest trendy cause, others were racist extremists who support terrorism and should have been locked up or deported.

They weren’t carrying banners calling for peace, but for Israel to be destroyed.

Chanting, “Death to the IDF” or, “Globalise the Intifada” is calling for Jewish people to be killed.

In the wake of the ambulances arson attack, London’s Mayor Sadiq Khan told us: “Londoners will never be cowed by this kind of hatred and intimidation.”

Where has he been for the last three years?



Jewish kids cover up religious symbols in case they’re attacked going to school, students harassed at university and patients hide their identity in hospital.

Synagogues attacked. Swastikas painted on homes, and businesses smashed up because their owners are Jewish.

Far too often the authorities have simply turned a blind eye.

That is why racist chanting and incitement was allowed on the marches for so long.

Or in Birmingham, why the police colluded with sectarian politicians, hate preachers and violent extremists to ban people from a Maccabi Tel Aviv v Aston Villa match.



Earlier this month, extremists on the annual Al Quds Day gathered to support the Iranian dictatorship that kidnaps British citizens, launches terror attacks on British bases and even here in the UK.

What are these traitors even doing here?

And this weekend, police in Kent refused to take any action over a grotesque exhibition depicting British Jews as evil baby killers and Jews drinking blood and secretly running the world.

On the rare occasion anyone is arrested and charged, the courts have failed to convict even violent thugs, let alone those accused of antisemitic incitement.

Over the last few years, Parliament debated Israel more than the NHS, crime, poverty or immigration, accusing it of the worst crimes possible.



MPs spend more time on a conflict thousands of miles away they can’t do anything about than jobs, schools or hospitals here in the UK.

Of course, it is possible to criticise Israel without being a racist, but if the only country you campaign against or want to abolish or boycott just happens to be the world’s only Jewish one, don’t tell me you’re not an antisemite.

It is the same story at the BBC, where they focus on Israel and Gaza more than any other conflict and cover it with the same bias and lack of balance.

This obsessive hatred of the world’s only Jewish state drives hostility towards people who are identified with Israel, which is the vast majority of Jewish people.

So I’m sick of empty promises to oppose antisemitism from people who single Israel out or hold it to standards never applied to other countries.



How can they tell us they are dealing with anti-Jewish racism at the same time as calling for boycotts, sanctions or embargoes that prevent Israel from defending its Jewish citizens?

Islamist extremism is obviously the biggest threat to social cohesion, but the 69-page strategy the Government published last week devotes just two sentences to discussing it.

Instead, they’re bringing in a new definition of anti-Muslim hatred which many fear will limit free speech and legitimate criticism of extremism.

Look how quickly left-wing MPs accuse everyone else of racism.

Why don’t they demand anti-Jewish racists and extremists are prosecuted with the same speed and determination as those who incited violence or racism after the murders in Southport?



And start standing up for our values.

People who hate Jews, support terrorism or think Israel should not be allowed to exist must not be allowed to come to Britain or stay here.

We’ve got enough extremists already, so if you don’t believe in democracy, equality and freedom, go and live somewhere else.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

Clamp down on extremist preachers spewing hatred in mosques, fire NHS staff or university lecturers responsible for racism and, finally, stop the hate marches taking over our streets once and for all.

Britain has beaten extremists and terrorists in the past — but if we don’t take urgent action, our country will be changed for ever.

News.title: High speed HS2 trains may be SLOWED DOWN in bid to stop £100billion project spiralling further out of control
News.content:
RAIL FAIL High speed HS2 trains may be SLOWED DOWN in bid to stop £100billion project spiralling further out of control

HS2 trains may be slowed down in a bid to stop the £100billion project spiralling further out of control.

Ministers are looking at cutting speeds on the London to Birmingham High Speed 2 route from a planned 224mph as delays and costs rise.



HS2 boss Mark Wild is expected to confirm the line will miss its 2033 deadline and be well over budget — though that update has been delayed until after May’s local authority elections.

The railway was designed for trains to go faster than any conventional line in the world.



But officials say it left the project difficult to deliver.

Testing trains at this speed would mean sending them abroad.

Building a test track would take years, at huge costs.

UK trains can currently run at 125mph top speed.

Transport Secretary Heidi Alexander is looking at ways to

get the railway open sooner and at lower cost for taxpayers.

An inside source said: “As we reset the project, we will take every opportunity to claw as much time and taxpayers’ money back as possible, as we deliver this railway line as fast as possible.”

News.title: NATO scrambles warplanes after horror Russian attack and threat to UK
News.content:
NATO scrambles warplanes after horror Russian attack and threat to UK

Vladimir Putin has staged massive new strikes on Ukraine forcing NATO to scramble warplanes from two allied countries in Europe.

It came as the Kremlin warned Britain of "dire consequences" over the use by the Ukrainian armed forces of UK-supplied Storm Shadow missiles to strike Russian territory.

This is a breaking news story.

News.title: Cost of vet treatment in UK to come down under major rule change
News.content:
Cost of vet treatment in UK to come down under major rule change

It has been called 'the most extensive review of veterinary services in a generation'

View 3 Images Vets will have to put a cap on some prices

Vets will be legally bound to capping prescription fees and publishing price lists among new measures which will start coming into force later this year, the competition watchdog has announced. The Competition and Markets Authority’s final reforms for the sector will help pet owners better navigate the vet services market, it said.

‌



Other legally binding measures will include a price comparison website and mandatory branding by large vet groups to boost competition and drive down prices. The CMA said pet owners using a vet practice that is part of a larger chain can expect to see changes before Christmas, including standard price lists.

‌



Martin Coleman, chairman of the Competition and Markets Authority’s independent inquiry group, said: “This is the most extensive review of veterinary services in a generation, and today’s reforms will make a real difference to the millions of pet owners who want the best for their pets but struggle to find the practice, treatment and price that meets their needs.

‌



View 3 Images You will get to see a list of prices

“Too often, people are left in the dark about who owns their practice, treatment options and prices – even when facing bills running into thousands of pounds.

‌



“Our measures mean it will be made clear to pet owners which practices are part of large groups, which are charging higher prices and fo r the first time, vet businesses will be held to account by an independent regulator.

View 3 Images Pet owners will know who owns their vet

“Our changes put pet owners at the centre but also help vets by enhancing trust in the profession and protecting clinical judgment from undue commercial pressure – and that is important to ensure our pets continue to get the best care.”

Article continues below

The CMA said practices must publish a comprehensive price list for standard services, including consultations, common procedures, diagnostics, written prescriptions and cremation options under its new rules. Prescriptions – for which “many” practices charge £30 or more for each – are to be capped at £21 for the first medicine and £12.50 for any additional medicines.

Practices must also provide a written estimate in advance for any treatment expected to cost £500 or more, including aftercare costs, as well as an itemised bill. Emergency care will be the only exception for written estimates.

Prices and information about who owns the surgery are to be made available to pet owners through the Royal College of Veterinary Surgeons (RCVS) ‘Find a Vet’ service, which will share the data with third-party comparison sites. Vet businesses must make it clear whether they are part of a group or an independent business, with details of group ownership to be displayed on signs at the surgery and online.

News.title: Iran in massive missile attack on Tel Aviv despite Trump peace talks claim
News.content:
Iran has launched a wave of missiles towards Israel just hours after Donald Trump claimed "very good and productive" talks were held with the country to resolve the ongoing Middle East conflict.

Air raid sirens were triggered across Israel, including Tel Aviv, this morning after a series of missiles were launched by Iran - which left six people lightly injured, Israeli police said. Officers said a munition carrying around 100 kilograms of explosives struck Tel Aviv, causing significant damage to buildings and vehicles.

One attack saw homes in northern Israel ripped apart by falling debris after an interception.

Image: AP) AP)

The strikes come just a day after Trump said he had postponed "any and all" military strikes against Iran's power plants following "very good and productive" talks - which he claimed were heading towards a "complete and total resolution of our hostilities" in the Middle East.

The US president earlier gave Iran an ultimatum, demanding the country ended its effective blockade on the Strait of Hormuz within 48 hours or the US would "obliterate" its power plants.

"If Iran doesn't FULLY OPEN, WITHOUT THREAT, the Strait of Hormuz, within 48 HOURS from this exact point in time, the United States of America will hit and obliterate their various POWER PLANTS, STARTING WITH THE BIGGEST ONE FIRST," he said in a post on Truth Social on Saturday.

Iran responded by issuing threats of its own, warning it will retaliate in four ways if the Trump were to follow through.

Following Trump's claims of peace talks with Iran, an X account attributed to the country's parliament speaker Mohammad-Bagher Ghalibaf said no negotiations had taken place with the US.

It said "fake news" has been used to "manipulate" the oil markets, and that the Iranian people "demand complete and remorseful punishment of the aggressors".

News.title: Tonga earthquake: Huge 7.5 magnitude tremor sparks terrifying tsunami alert
News.content:
Residents of a country have been given a terrifying warning after a massive 7.5-magnitude earthquake has struck in the South Pacific Ocean.

People in Tonga have been told to move to higher ground and other countries in the region are concerned over a tsunami danger.

The United States Geological Survey said the quake struck early Tuesday evening local time at a depth of about 148 miles. Earthquakes at shallower depths are felt more strongly at the surface.

Ensure our latest headlines always appear at the top of your Google Search by making us a Preferred Source. Click here to activate or add us as your Preferred Source in your Google search settings.

Image: United States Geological Survey) United States Geological Survey)





The jolt was centred at sea, 95 miles west of Neiafu, the second largest town in the island nation but there were no immediate reports of damage.

The island nation is about 1,100 miles northeast of New Zealand whose National Emergency Management Agency said it was assessing whether the earthquake has created a tsunami that could affect New Zealand.

"If a tsunami has been generated in this location, it is not likely to arrive in New Zealand for at least two hours," it said.

Tonga’s National Disaster Risk Management Office warned all in the low-lying island nation to move immediately to higher ground or inland. People should avoid beaches, shorelines, and low-lying coastal areas until an all-clear was given, said a post on the office’s Facebook page.

The USGS did not issue a tsunami warning for the region, and the Pacific Tsunami Warning Center in Hawaii said in a bulletin that there was no tsunami threat because the quake was “located too deep inside the earth.”

Tonga is an archipelago in Polynesia made up of 171 islands with just over 100,000 people, most of whom live on the main island of Tongatapu. Tuesday’s quake was centred nearer to the Vava’u island group.

A person at the Tanoa International Dateline Hotel on the beachfront at Nuku’alofa, which is on Tongatapu, said she wasn’t aware of any damage. “The whole building shaked. No further damage. Everything was okay,” she said.

Tonga is situated on the Pacific Ring of Fire, the arc of seismic faults where much of the world’s earthquake and volcanic activity occurs. A tsunami set off by a volcanic eruption in 2022 killed three people.

News.title: Mum 'chilled to the core' after finding out what glow in baby's eye meant
News.content:
Mum 'chilled to the core' after finding out what glow in baby's eye meant

Doctors had reassured her everything was fine

View 6 Images Maggie had a problem with both of her eyes

A mum has told how she spotted her daughter's cancer that even doctors had missed after seeing a beige glow in her eye. Just weeks after giving birth to little Maggie, mum, Jennifer Salt, noticed that her newborn’s left eye was swollen and “gunky”.

‌



Despite reassurance from doctors, the 40-year-old had concerns; which were only made worse as her baby's eye began showing a beige-coloured glow. After multiple doctor's visits, an ultrasound showed that the now four-year-old’s eye had a cloud-like structure. And it was cancer.

‌



“It was completely shattering,” said Jennifer, a project manager, from Chesterfield, Derbyshire. “Then, things were made worse, as we were told it was present in both eyes. Instead of letting it defeat me, I went into autopilot mode. I was determined to fight for my little girl.”

‌



View 6 Images One of Maggie's eyes could not be saved

Jennifer had noticed that Maggie had trouble focusing just months before her diagnosis in November 2021. She shared her concerns with partner, Karl, 39, who agreed that something was gravely wrong. When researching her daughter’s symptoms, though, the mum-of-two had a hunch of what to expect next.

She said: “What I read chilled me to the core. I panicked that Maggie would go blind. A referral would take four weeks, which made my heart sink. I reached out to our local MP and told them how nobody was listening to me. Thankfully, it worked and we got the emergency appointment confirming everything I had feared.”

‌



Maggie, who has no vision in her left eye, has bilateral retinoblastoma. In her right eye, it was Grade B, while in the left, it was Grade E.

View 6 Images Doctors had missed the signs at first

At four months old, she had her first round of chemotherapy to shrink the tumours. Sadly, as the left eye had shrunk so much as a result of treatment, they were left with no option but to remove it for good. Jennifer said: “Watching the ordeal Maggie was going through took a toll on me. My mental health plummeted.

‌



“Her body rejected the implant, so the next option was to carry out a graft by taking fat from her bottom and inserting it into her eye. It just kept growing cysts, however. I questioned why nothing was working and felt like the world was punishing me. I couldn’t bear seeing her go through much more.”

View 6 Images Maggie needed multiple rounds of treatment

In another devastating blow, it was found that Maggie had a mass on her pineal gland in the centre of her brain. Thankfully, it was only a cyst. Despite trying to give Maggie a “normal” childhood, people often stare and make disgusted remarks.

‌



She said: “It was devastating to see her so self-conscious and fearful. “I tried to let her know how beautiful and clever she was each day. When we got her a more realistic prosthetic eye, her confidence boosted; just in time for school. And, luckily, nobody seemed to notice at first.”

View 6 Images Maggie may still deal with tumours until she reaches six years old

Due to the type of cancer, Maggie may still deal with tumours until she reaches six years old – as well as other types of cancer. Jennifer hopes to share their ordeal – and the warning signs – so other parents are aware before it’s too late.

‌



She added: “I’m so grateful for all the help we’ve received. Coping with a member of the family having cancer, particularly a child, is so gruelling. I hope there will be specific support given to families in the future for all the trauma they’re going through.

View 6 Images Maggie has the biggest smile

“I know it would’ve meant a lot to me; and still would if it were offered now. Maggie has had sclerotherapy to treat the cysts in her eye socket and is currently awaiting further treatment. But we’re not sure when that will be. We don’t know how much vision she has in her right eye yet, but we know that she’s got a significant amount.

Article continues below

“She’s got the biggest smile and such a zest for life.”

News.title: 'I'll never get over what I found inside chest of drawers from charity shop'
News.content:
'I'll never get over what I found inside chest of drawers from charity shop'

People are taking to Reddit to share the weird and bizarre things they have found inside their charity shop purchases - and one item discovered was absolutely wild.

View 3 Images People are revealing all the bizarre things they've found inside items purchased from charity shops (Image: Getty)

Charity shops - those enchanting, labyrinthine treasure troves filled with affordable gems.

‌



Typically packed with an eclectic mix of clothes, footwear, books, trinkets, furniture, crockery, accessories, and everyday items - charity shops offer a thrifty way to shop, contribute to society, and counteract the relentless cycle of fast fashion and its environmental impact.

‌



People frequently use charity shops as a means to declutter their homes and wardrobes, donating items they no longer need. These items are then purchased by someone else who breathes new life into them, and so the cycle continues.

‌



In this process, people often overlook cleaning out or fully emptying the items they donate, leading to new owners occasionally discovering pleasant little surprises when they delve into a pocket or open a drawer.

View 3 Images They couldn't believe their eyes (Image: Getty)

A thread on Reddit delves into this very topic, with users sharing the bizarre, delightful, and sometimes downright odd things they've unearthed in their charity shop purchases, reports the Express.

‌



On the subreddit AskUK, one Redditor posed the intriguing question, "What's the strangest thing you've found inside a charity shop purchase?", and the responses were quite extraordinary.

In fact, the most outrageous response came from the original poster (OP) themselves, with the Reddit user disclosing how they discovered an... unsavoury item in a piece of furniture they had bought from a charity shop.

The original poster shared: "I was thinking back to my time in New Zealand, and the purchase of a $50 chest of drawers. Upon getting it home, we found $50 and a bag of heroin in one of the drawers. So, I ask what is the strangest find in a charity shop purchase?"

‌



One Reddit user couldn't conceal their astonishment at the discovery: "Seriously? A jacket or a pair of jeans I could maybe understand, but if your shop's stocking a chest of drawers surely you'd have a cursory glance inside the drawers first?"

Another remained completely unfazed: "Considering that Kiwi OP shops are essentially what UK charity shops used to be like 20 years ago, that doesn't surprise me."

A further contributor recounted their own chest of drawers discovery, which proved equally extraordinary: "I bought a chest of drawers from a charity shop, absolutely s*** my y-fronts when I opened the bottom drawer and there was a glass eye in the back of the drawer wedged at the back underneath the paper people used to put in drawer bottoms."

‌



View 3 Images Charity shops are an affordable treasure trove of items (Image: Getty)

Additional users joined the discussion, revealing the similarly peculiar items they had discovered whilst browsing charity shops.

One individual stumbled upon an unusual assortment: "I bought a sideboard from a charity shop and found an entire set of cutlery in a drawer, opera glasses, and an old cast iron iron wedged inside."

‌



Meanwhile, another volunteer had discovered something truly unique: "I used to volunteer for a charity shop, and they once received a donation of a briefcase, and inside it was the previous owner's last will and testament. The family returned the next day looking for it thankfully."

In a disturbing revelation, one Reddit user shared: "My friend used to work at OXFAM and once opened a bag of donated clothes and found a dead cat in with them." While there is absolutely no verification for this claim, it's equal parts horrifying and heartbreaking.

Some people though, appear blessed with extraordinary fortune, with one user recounting their charity shop surprise discovery: "My ex worked in Oxfam and picked me up a handbag he knew of like. Going through it I found €200 in a pocket."

‌



Another delighted charity-shop visitor said: "Went for a really nice coat for my kid for £5 and she put her hand in the inside pocket and pulled out £28 worth of gift tokens that were still valid."

There were also some individuals who found themselves on the opposite side of the situation - being the ones who left something precious in an item they donated to a charity shop.

One user admitted: "I donated a jacket forgetting that I hid an unusual diamond, sapphire and white gold ring in the pocket. I hope that whoever acquired the jacket appreciated the ring as much as I did."

‌



And in what can only be described as a remarkably peculiar (and frankly unsettling) incident, one Redditor shared: "Not me, but my mum.

"Years ago, one of her friends from somewhere down south passed away. We were on holiday in India at the time and didn't make it back home for the funeral (they weren't very close, but mum was still a little sad about it).

"About five-ish years later, we were on a little weekend away in Scotland. We were ambling about in a charity shop looking at books, and mum buys one - which was odd cos she hates reading fiction.

Article continues below

"When we get back to our bnb, she flicks through it and a bookmark falls out. The bookmark was a memorial prayer card from the funeral she missed. She was in mild shock for the rest of the trip and kept both the card and the book to this day.

"...also she never did read the book."

News.title: Katie Piper on 'true care' as Brits 'happy to try anything' for better skin
News.content:
Katie Piper on 'true care' as Brits 'happy to try anything' for better skin

Model and presenter Katie Piper has revealed the simple hack she relies on for better skin as research uncovers a series of quirky DIY skincare techniques UK adults have tried

View 3 Images Katie Piper has opened up about how she keeps her skin glowing (Image: PinPep)

Brits have confessed to using these top 20 unusual skincare tricks - which mainly rely on natural ingredients found in kitchen cupboards. These include placing teabags on puffy eyes, using egg yolk to treat rough skin and even applying baking soda as an exfoliant.

‌



Research involving 2,000 adults uncovered a range of intriguing DIY beauty treatments, such as using avocado as a face scrub, hand treatments made from strawberries and applying green tea as a skin rinse. Others use lemon juice to brighten skin, indulge in oatmeal baths and apply yoghurt as a facemask, all in pursuit of healthier-looking skin.

‌



View 3 Images The presenter and model has partner with Surcare to highlight how far Brits will go in the name of better skin (Image: PinPep)

‌



Around 15% revealed they use these methods at least once a week, while 7% admitted they're willing to try 'anything' that might enhance their appearance.

The study was commissioned by laundry detergent brand Surcare, which has appointed Katie Piper OBE as their brand and wellness consultant, to highlight how often people overlook the impact clothing can have when it touches skin.

She said: "When you think about how often our skin is in contact with clothing, bedding and towels, it becomes clear that skincare doesn't start in the bathroom, it starts with what touches your skin every day.

‌



"When we wash our clothes, traces of the detergent can remain in the fibres, and those fabrics stay in close contact with our skin for much of the day. In many ways, fabric acts as our second skin, so how we care for it really matters.

"Small, thoughtful choices - like using dermatologically approved laundry products - can help support your skin without adding complexity or risk."

The research revealed three in 10 turn to DIY remedies because they contain natural ingredients, 19% to steer clear of harsh chemicals and 21% because they're more affordable than shop-bought alternatives.

‌



View 3 Images A third have changed laundry detergent to boost skin health (Image: PinPep)

It emerges as the typical respondent confessed to splashing out £146 annually on skincare items. This rises to £205 for Millennials – the highest across all age groups – in contrast to merely £80 for Baby Boomers.

Primarily, they're used to ward off skin problems (49%), though 33% use them for a feeling of self-care and wellness, whilst nearly 4% turn to them because influencers and celebrities do.

‌



Overall, 56% of respondents have suffered from a skin complaint or condition, such as eczema, dermatitis, acne or sensitive skin.

Applying skincare products such as creams or serums (48%) is the most popular approach to tackle these concerns, followed by increasing water intake (45%) or obtaining prescription medication from a GP (36%).

Meanwhile, 26% rely on supplements, 24% have attempted altering their diet – and 30% have changed laundry detergent to boost skin health.

‌



However, more than half (52%) don't think about what washing detergent they use as part of their skincare regime to prevent irritation, and only 75% are confident their detergent is appropriate for maintaining healthy skin.

Despite this, participants typically spend nearly 16 hours in contact with fabrics each day, according to the OnePoll.com research.

Katie Piper added: "From toothpaste on spots to apply honey as a face mask, people often experiment with different beauty trends in the hope of better skin, but I've learned that true care is usually found in simple, everyday choices that support your wellbeing over time.

Article continues below

"For me, that includes thinking beyond traditional skincare - I've always believed in simple, preventative care, and sometimes it's not about adding more products, but trying to remove irritation.

"Choosing laundry formulations that are kind to skin, without fragrances, dyes or enzymes, is just as important as the skincare you apply directly."

TOP 20 SKINCARE HACKS TRIED BY BRITS:

News.title: Donald Trump's unhinged 24 hours as he gets stumped by simple question at Elvis' Graceland mansion
News.content:
Donald Trump's unhinged 24 hours as he gets stumped by simple question at Elvis' Graceland mansion

Trump fulfilled his lifelong dream of getting a taxpayer funded VIP visit to a tourist attraction, while his war of choice raged on in Iran

View 3 Images Trump visiting Graceland and putting things into, perhaps too much, perspective (Image: AFP via Getty Images)

Donald Trump's presidency is full of ethical and philosophical puzzles.

‌



For example, today we were forced to decide which was worse - The US President spending the day ordering a series of military strikes that would probably constitute war crimes, or him spending a good chunk of the day fanning about at Elvis Presley's house while there's a war on.

‌



We were also presented with the question: Would Jesus pass the SAVE America Act?

‌



And once again we were confronted with the metaphysical mystery of what happened to all those people who so loudly complained that Joe Biden was a bit old and doddery, aged 79, and have been eerily quiet about Donald Trump falling asleep in meetings, also aged 79.

Meanwhile in Trumpworld

Trump Goes to Graceland, Graceland, Memphis Tennessee

Trump claims to have cut a deal with Iran - and we'll find out if it's true next time the markets close

Trump dropped off during a live TV meeting

And he took the lord's name in vain to try and pressure congress

Trump's new DHS chief defended hitting kids with a belt

‌



Here's what you need to know....

1. Glory, Glory Hallelujah, His Truth (Social) is Marching On

We all know Trump has a fondness for Kings. He wants to be one, after all. We weren't, however, previously familiar with his love of THE King.

While in Memphis, Tennessee (more of which later, Trump paid a longer than is ideal visit to Graceland, the former home of one Elvis Aaron Presley.

‌



View 3 Images Trump signs a replica of one of Elvis' guitars. (Image: AFP via Getty Images)

"I love Elvis", Trump said during his VIP tour of the attraction. But he was stumped when asked by a reporter what his favourite Elvis song was.

"He's got so many. There's very few I don't like," he said.

‌



Which, weirdly, is an almost identical answer to the one he gives when asked for his favourite Bible verse. Trump also claims to be a big fan of god.

Still, he got to hang around in Presley's "Jungle Room", and got to point at the posthumous Presidential Medal of Freedom he awarded to Elvis in 2018, during his first term.

He later posted a split image of his and Presley's faces, claiming people often tell him he looks like Elvis. He does not.

‌



View 3 Images Trump possibly pretending to be the ghost of Elvis, it's unclear (Image: AP)

2. Dozy Donald gets snoozy

Before Trump's random pilgrimage to the home of a dead singer, Trump was in Memphis for a roundtable on the Memphis Safe Task Force - one of his city invasions, where he deployed troops (who are not legally allowed to perform police functions) to crack down on crime.

He was surrounded by local officials and members of his cabinet, all of whom waxed at length about how successful the task force had been, and how clever and handsome he had been to come up with it.

‌



But no amount of flattery is enough to keep a nearly 80 year old man from Queens awake in a warm room.

His head dipped and he took what will probably be dismissed as very extended blinks during the lengthy event.

Content cannot be displayed without consent

‌



3. Do it for Jesus

Trump invoked the name of Jesus Christ in a bid to pressure congress to pass his election rigging plan.

He said he was going to order Republicans to withhold funding from the Department of Homeland Security until the Senate passes his bill to pass the so-called "SAVE America Act".

The act would demand voters show photo ID to register - something millions of eligible voters do not possess.

‌



He also wants to ban postal and absentee voting, demanding the vast majority of Americans vote in person, and on the same day.

The bill also allows him to seize state-held voter rolls - which many fear he will use to sow doubt in the result of November's midterm election, allowing him to make false claims of fraud.

Desperate to get the bill passed, Trump has demanded Republicans tack it to funding for the Department for Homeland Security (DHS) - something already causing chaos across the United States.

‌



Democrats had already held up funding for the department, demanding changes to operating rules for ICE agents, banning them from covering their faces and ensuring they wear body cameras on duty.

Republicans have refused to approve that move, causing huge delays at airports across the country, where security staff are paid by DHS.

And Trump today demanded the Senate stay in Washington DC over the Easter break to ensure the hugely controversial bill gets passed - even suggesting they do so "for Jesus".

‌



"So I'm tying homeland security into voter identifications with picture and proof of citizenship in order to vote," he said.

"Don't worry about Easter, going home. Make this one for Jesus, ok?"

Content cannot be displayed without consent

‌



4. Trump announces he'll only bomb Iran on the weekends

Trump announced this morning that there had been a breakthrough in talks with Iran, so he wouldn't have to bomb civilian energy infrastructure - the war crime-skirting punishment he threatened the regime with if they didn't open up the Strait of Hormuz.

Trump claims his administration has been talking to Iran's people, and that everything was going to get smoothed out. He gave a deadline of Friday, otherwise bombing would recommence.

Strategically, this benefits Trump in two ways. First, the financial markets close on the weekend, so they might have a few days of relative calm before he crashes them again.

‌



Second, it gives him time to rally reinforcements. The US has more ships and marines on the way to the Middle East, who are, entirely coincidentally, going to get there somewhere around Friday.

For what I'd worth, Iran deny having held talks with Trump, and accused him of retreating. It seems like the guy the Trump administration is talking to is Mohammad ⁠Bagher Ghalibaf, the Speaker of the Iranian Parliament, who is suddenly in contention to be the Trump-installed leader of Iran.

Anyway, JD Vance and (who else?) Steve Witkoff are reportedly going to meet with Iranian representaatives in the coming days. What could go wrong?

‌



5. Trump's new Homeland Security chief defended hitting kids with a belt

Kristi Noem, famously, boasted about shooting a puppy with a dog in her memoir.

But Markwayne Mullin isn't one to be overshadowed by an objectionable personal anecdote.

Footage of Mullin has emerged of remarks he made to a Church Group in 2022. In the clips, Mullin defends corporal punishment for children, admitting he had been whipped with a belt by his father, and suggesting his abusive dad had been right to assault him with a weapon.

‌



Likewise, Mullin admitted, he had spanked his own kids, arguing it made them fear disappointing him and kept them on the straight and narrow.

There's a whole thread of these videos and they're well worth a watch.

Content cannot be displayed without consent

News.title: Chris Mason: Reeves to set out principles on who might get energy bills support - and who won't
News.content: With the Conservatives, Reform UK and some Labour MPs calling for new drilling for oil and gas to be authorised in the North Sea, the Energy Secretary Ed Miliband told a meeting of Labour backbenchers last night: "Anyone who tells you that new licences in the North Sea will make any difference to price is not telling you the truth. Because gas is bought and sold on the international market and the price is set there."

News.title: Vet prescription fees to be capped at £21
News.content: As part of the reforms, pet owners must be told they can have a written prescription which could save them money. The fee for these prescriptions will be capped at £21 for the first medicine, and at £12.50 for any additional medicines.

News.title: Royal Mail staff say they were told to hide post to look like delivery targets met
News.content: "Nothing has really improved, it's gotten worse," one said. "It feels each week that the line managers are looking for a new way to blame any failures of the new delivery system on the posties, rather than looking at what the failures actually are."

News.title: UK's transplant system was world-leading - now it lags behind other Western nations
News.content: While the number of organs donated in the UK is equal to, or better than, much of other parts of Europe per head, the NHS uses far fewer of the hearts and lungs it gets than most other countries. Just one in 10 lungs and one in seven hearts are transplanted. Some countries make use of twice as many.

News.title: 'Like the plane got cut in half': LaGuardia crash that killed two pilots now under investigation
News.content: "We have one air traffic control specialist who was in line with TSA for three hours until we called in Houston to beg to see if we can get her through, so we can get her here," Homendy said during her news conference in New York.

News.title: Police investigating possible Iran link to attack on Jewish charity ambulances
News.content: Run by volunteers, it has served the north London community of Golders Green, which has a large Jewish population, since 1979. There are dozens of synagogues throughout the area and according to the London Data Store, 49% of residents in the Golders Green ward identify as Jewish.

News.title: Heat pumps for all new homes and plug-in solar in green tech drive
News.content: The guidance published on Tuesday means that from 2028, no new homes will be on the gas network - and will instead be on a heat network or get a heat pump - and they must have solar panels on their roofs covering an area equivalent to 40% of the ground floor space.
